description: Real-world edge case companies for stress-testing HubSpot parent-child routing. Each category targets a specific weakness in the 4-signal Smart Compare detection system.

categories.description: Multiple subsidiaries share the parent's email domain. Core use case for the routing system.

companies.notes: 32 teams, all league-office employees use @nfl.com. Team-specific employees may also use @nfl.com for league functions.

test_scenarios.description: Contact LinkedIn points to subsidiary — direct match
test_scenarios.contact_headline: VP Operations at Kansas City Chiefs

test_scenarios.description: LinkedIn shows parent but title contains subsidiary name
test_scenarios.contact_title: Director of Entertainment Teams - Kansas City Chiefs

test_scenarios.description: Generic title but headline mentions subsidiary
test_scenarios.contact_headline: Senior Marketing Manager at Dallas Cowboys | NFL

test_scenarios.description: Genuinely works for NFL league office — no false rescue
No LinkedIn, generic title — cannot determine

companies.notes: Most Alphabet subsidiaries use @google.com email. Some like Waymo and Verily have own domains but many employees still use @google.com.

test_scenarios.contact_headline: PM at Verily Life Sciences | Alphabet

companies.notes: Corporate employees use @disney.com. Some subs like ESPN have @espn.com but many cross-functional roles use the parent domain.

test_scenarios.contact_headline: VP Finance at The Walt Disney Company

companies.notes: NBCUniversal employees use @nbcuni.com. Comcast corporate uses @comcast.com. Cross-pollination between entities.
Most subsidiaries use @amazon.com. AWS has distinct LinkedIn presence but shares email domain. Whole Foods employees may use @wholefoods.com.

test_scenarios.contact_title: Head of Original Content - Prime Video

categories.description: Employees list the parent holding company on LinkedIn rather than the actual subsidiary they work for. Tests Title Rescue and Multi-Role signals.

companies.notes: Agency employees frequently list WPP on LinkedIn for brand recognition, even when employed by a specific agency. Subsidiaries have own domains (ogilvy.com, groupm.com) but many use @wpp.com.

test_scenarios.contact_title: Chief Strategy Officer, Ogilvy North America

test_scenarios.contact_headline: ECD at Wunderman Thompson | WPP Agency

companies.notes: Similar to WPP — agency employees list Publicis Groupe for prestige. Publicis Sapient and Epsilon are tech-focused subs with distinct identities.

test_scenarios.contact_headline: Data Science Leader at Epsilon | Publicis Groupe

companies.notes: After 2023 brand consolidation, many sub-brands merged under 'Dentsu' umbrella. Employees who worked at Merkle now list Dentsu — but Merkle still operates as a distinct entity.
Accenture Song (formerly Accenture Interactive) is a mega-agency. Avanade is a joint venture with Microsoft. Droga5 is a creative agency. All employees typically list just 'Accenture' on LinkedIn.
IPG is the holding company. Sub-agencies have strong independent brands. Employees may list either IPG or the specific agency.

test_scenarios.contact_headline: Design Director at R/GA | IPG Company

categories.description: Parent and subsidiaries have similar, abbreviated, or commonly confused names. Tests that exact-substring matching doesn't false-positive.

companies.notes: Extreme naming confusion. 'JPMorgan' vs 'J.P. Morgan' vs 'Chase' vs 'JPMorgan Chase'. Investment bank is 'J.P. Morgan', retail is 'Chase'. Employees use @jpmorgan.com across all entities.

test_scenarios.description: Title says 'Chase' — should match Chase subsidiary, NOT 'JPMorgan Chase' parent
test_scenarios.risk: Could false-match to parent 'JPMorgan Chase' if substring matching isn't precise

test_scenarios.description: Title uses 'J.P. Morgan' with periods — mapping table has 'J.P. Morgan'
test_scenarios.contact_title: Managing Director, J.P. Morgan Asset Management

test_scenarios.description: 'JPMorgan' appears in both parent and child names — must disambiguate
test_scenarios.risk: Generic 'JPMorgan' reference — cannot determine specific subsidiary

companies.notes: 'Ernst & Young' vs 'EY' — same company, two names. 'EY-Parthenon' is strategy consulting arm. Regional entities are separate legal entities.

test_scenarios.description: 'EY' appears in parent AND subsidiary names — substring match on 'EY' would match everything
test_scenarios.risk: Short abbreviation 'EY' would false-match 'EY-Parthenon' if matching isn't bounded

test_scenarios.description: Title explicitly mentions 'EY-Parthenon' — should match

companies.notes: 'PricewaterhouseCoopers' vs 'PwC' — official rebrand to PwC. 'Strategy&' (formerly Booz & Company) is a distinct subsidiary with a special character in the name.

test_scenarios.description: 'Strategy&' contains '&' — tests regex/string matching with special characters
test_scenarios.risk: Ampersand in name may break substring matching

test_scenarios.description: Contact uses old name 'PricewaterhouseCoopers' — mapping table has 'PwC'
test_scenarios.risk: Old name vs new name mismatch — mapping table must include aliases

companies.notes: Post-rebrand confusion. 'Meta' is parent, 'Facebook' is now a product/subsidiary. Employees use @meta.com but may list 'Facebook' in title.

test_scenarios.description: 'Facebook' in title could mean the product team OR the legacy company entity
test_scenarios.risk: Is 'Facebook' a subsidiary or a product? Mapping table definition matters.

companies.notes: Subsidiaries operate extremely independently with their own domains. Unlike other conglomerates, employees rarely use the parent domain. Tests the case where domain mismatch is NORMAL.

test_scenarios.description: GEICO employee uses @geico.com, not @berkshirehathaway.com — no mismatch to detect
test_scenarios.risk: System should NOT flag this as mismatch — subsidiary has its own domain

test_scenarios.description: Rare case: corporate staffer at parent entity uses @berkshirehathaway.com

categories.description: Multiple internal divisions/business units operate under one corporate domain. Tests Multi-Role signal and division-level routing.

companies.notes: Largest single-domain conglomerate. AWS alone has 200+ services. Cross-divisional transfers are common.

test_scenarios.description: Contact has roles in both Amazon corporate and AWS
No secondary LinkedIn role — division name only in title

companies.notes: LinkedIn and GitHub maintain separate domains. Xbox, Azure, Nuance employees typically use @microsoft.com. Post-Activision acquisition adds complexity.
Slack maintains slack.com domain for some employees. Tableau and MuleSoft employees mostly use @salesforce.com post-acquisition. Heroku is being wound down.
Aggressive acquirer. Many brands maintain separate identities. Splunk (acquired 2024) is the largest recent addition.
Internal divisions (Business vs Consumer) plus acquired brands (TracFone, Visible). All use @verizon.com.

categories.description: US-based companies with international subsidiaries as separate legal entities. Tests Region → Type → Segment → Owner decision tree.

companies.notes: Regional entities are separate legal entities for tax/compliance. Employees use @salesforce.com globally. LinkedIn may show local entity or global parent.

test_scenarios.description: Contact LinkedIn shows regional entity
LinkedIn shows global parent, title specifies region

companies.notes: Oracle has 200+ legal entities worldwide. NetSuite operates semi-independently. Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) is a product division, not a legal entity.
German parent with US as largest market. SAP Labs in India/China are R&D centers. Qualtrics was re-acquired after brief IPO. Concur maintains brand identity.
Red Hat (acquired 2019) maintains @redhat.com domain and independent LinkedIn. IBM Consulting is a major division. Regional entities are large (IBM India has 100K+ employees).

test_scenarios.description: Red Hat employee uses @redhat.com — NOT a domain mismatch
test_scenarios.risk: System should recognize redhat.com as an IBM subsidiary domain, not flag as unrelated

companies.notes: Each country entity is a separate legal firm under the Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Limited (DTTL) umbrella. LinkedIn shows 'Deloitte' for nearly all employees regardless of country.

categories.description: Companies acquired in 2024-2025 transitioning to parent domain/brand. Tests whether the mapping table stays current during brand transitions.

companies.notes: VMware employees transitioning to @broadcom.com emails. LinkedIn profiles inconsistently updated. Some still show VMware, others Broadcom. Brand 'VMware by Broadcom' in use.

test_scenarios.description: Contact still has VMware LinkedIn, but email changed to @broadcom.com
test_scenarios.risk: Mapping table must have VMware as a Broadcom subsidiary — may not be there yet

test_scenarios.description: Contact updated LinkedIn to Broadcom, but email still @vmware.com (transition period)
test_scenarios.risk: Domain vmware.com now belongs to Broadcom — domain mismatch detection must know this

companies.notes: Splunk brand maintained under Cisco. Email transition ongoing. Some employees use @splunk.com, others @cisco.com.

test_scenarios.contact_headline: SIEM at Splunk | A Cisco Company
test_scenarios.risk: splunk.com domain not yet in parent domain list — may miss the Cisco relationship

companies.notes: Very recent acquisition. Juniper employees still fully on @juniper.net. LinkedIn profiles haven't changed. Brand integration barely started.

test_scenarios.description: Employee still fully on old domain and LinkedIn — mapping table probably doesn't have this yet
test_scenarios.risk: If mapping table doesn't have juniper.net as HPE domain, these contacts get misrouted entirely

companies.notes: Massive financial services merger. Both brands will likely coexist for years. Two entirely separate CRM contact databases being merged.

test_scenarios.risk: Discover contacts flowing into Capital One's HubSpot — need mapping table entry

companies.notes: Announced 2025, Google's largest acquisition. Wiz employees will likely get @google.com emails eventually. Until then, @wiz.io domain is unlinked in mapping tables.

test_scenarios.risk: wiz.io not yet in any parent's domain list — contact will be orphaned until mapping table updated

test_scenarios.description: After transition: Wiz employee now on @google.com, LinkedIn still shows Wiz
test_scenarios.contact_headline: Cloud Security at Wiz | Google Cloud
test_scenarios.risk: Mapping table must be updated to include Wiz as Google/Alphabet subsidiary with wiz-inc LinkedIn

categories_by_difficulty: linkedin_profile_mismatches (common — Title Rescue must be reliable)
